item.title: 台積電是美國的！？台積電如何崛起？How did TSMC become a leading chip manufacturer?|#我的學習筆記 #520 @mynotebooks
item.itunes:title: 台積電是美國的！？台積電如何崛起？How did TSMC become a leading chip manufacturer?|#我的學習筆記 #520 @mynotebooks
item.itunes:author: 我的學習筆記
item.itunes:summary:
Hello! Hello, everyone! I'm a niu ge. Whenever people talk about Korea. The first company that comes to mind. Probably either Samsung or LG. And whenever we talk about Japan. SONY might come to mind. Or Yamaha or something like that. When it comes to us in Taiwan. Which company comes to mind? There should be 87% of them. I think it's TSMC. That's right. So today's story. It's about TSMC. How did he go from a. small, unheard-of business. Becoming a global leader in semiconductors. The name “Protector of the Nation” was given to the company. In addition to its world-class achievements. It's also because it's recognized. It is to the world. It's an indispensable presence. Helps protect the status quo in the Republic of China. Seems to be recognized as representing the Republic of China. Achievements in political democratization and social modernization. and a free market economy. Outstanding Representatives of the Private Sector. And what about that Yulon you mentioned? Uh...I'm afraid of getting sued. So let's talk today. The mountain that protects the nation. And the one that's been circulating on the Internet for a long time. TSMC is MSMC. But is that really the case? Let's start with the founding of TSMC. TSMC's Founding. Roughly February 1987, before the end of martial law. It's the same as the foundation. From Taiwan's pre-war industrial policy. Leaders of the Construction Industry at the Time. and the Chinese in China and abroad. It's based on something like an ode to the Republic of China. An ode to Chinese nationalism. Wanting to promote industry to save the country. At the same time it can be said to be a. A true public sector project. A Public Sector Initiative. But when we first. In order to avoid the rigidity of the state-run business norms. That's why the official government shareholding is less than half. It also has a significant impact on TSMC's future operations. There's not a lot of interference. The only thing I can say is that it's good that it's not state-run. Otherwise, we wouldn't have the Protectorate today. TSMC was founded in 1987. Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). It's also known as the Industrial Technology Research Institute. A spin-off of ITRI's Electronics Research Institute. But TSMC was actually. Promoted by the Executive Yuan at the beginning. Phase II of the Semiconductor Long-Term Program. Graduates of Phase I. UMC is also quite famous. Also known as UMC. It was famous in 1980. And ITRI, which is committed to the program. was established in 1973. In other words. Before TSMC was founded. In fact, the relevant departments in Taiwan. have been around for more than 20 years. It's been paving the way. A Program to Promote the Development. Of Taiwan's Semiconductor Industry. A typical forward-looking industrial policy. During the time of the Republic of China's relocation to Taiwan. Taiwan is still a.

A typical colonial economy dependent on rice and sugar exports. Rice and sugar accounted for over 70% of exports. Luckily. After the war through a successful industrial policy. By the end of the 1960s. Taiwan's economy had successfully undergone. Initial industrialization. By 1970. Industrial goods already accounted for nearly 80% of exports. The share of rice and sugar has dropped to 3%. In the early stages of industrialization. It is not difficult to choose the target industry. You can refer to the development history of advanced countries. Firstly, the textile industry was the main light industry. For the people's livelihood. And then the upstream steel and petrochemical industries. But as we move forward. have caught up with the advanced countries. What's next? The officials in charge at the time. They all had a sense of worry. Worrying from time to time. What will be the new industries in Taiwan in 20 years? That's why the ministers of economic affairs at that time. Li Kuo-ting and Sun Yun-suan. Responsible for leading the Economic. And Construction Department. Thinking about where Taiwan's next new industry will be. And at that time, the U.S. was in the midst of a rapidly developing. The Emerging Electronic Information Industry. Looks like a promising target. The organization promoting industrial policy at the time. And the system was pretty much in place. The top political echelons are also keeping an eye. On the development. But in the process of promoting. Almost everything. has met with considerable resistance. Including the establishment. Of the Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). First proposal to promote the IC industry. Years later, the establishment of TSMC was proposed. Plan to operate a specialized foundry. All of which have been met with strong opposition. Like spending so much money on an investment. Will there be a return or something like that? But Sun is Sun. He's got it all figured out. It took more than a year. It convinced the old countrymen at the time. Getting the Legislature to agree. Finally succeeded in getting the consortium to agree to. Establishment of Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). Simultaneous. Mr. Sun and others have also been actively promoting. The Development of Taiwan's Electronics Industry. But such a new technology. You can't figure it out on your own. So Sun and the best of the best in the United States. Poon Wen-yuan jointly promoted the “Integrated Circuit Project”. He also convened the meeting with Mr. Chin-Tai Shih,. Mr. Ting-Yuan Yang, Mr. Ching-Chu Chang, and others. A group of young scholars joined the program. With Radio Corporation of America (RCA). Technology Transfer Cooperation. In a team of 19 people. We belong to design, manufacturing. Testing, equipment and other 4 groups. They came all the way to the United States. RCA Headquarters “Across the Sea”. A Practical Look at Semiconductor Manufacturing. And how a factory works. The team at the time. In addition to the short period of time. I'm just trying to absorb everything. It is also necessary to regularly report progress to the mainland. Purchase and build a plant in Taiwan. Process Equipment Required.

Yes, sir! You heard right. And while they're at it. ITRI was already working on it. Building Taiwan's First Wafer Demonstration Plant. Waiting for everyone to come back. We're ready to go. It's really fierce. It's not like some officials. I came back from a trip abroad with three big words. I'm impressed. 1977. ITRI's Institute of Electronics has set up. Integrated Circuit Demonstration Factory. 7.5 micron process. As a Primary Target. In December of that year. The yield rate was very good. Even exceeded RCA's estimate. And soon after. The factory successfully received the order. And so it begins. The era of self-produced integrated circuits. And many others. They're also actively contributing to the development. For example, Ronny Fei was the Undersecretary of Transportation. Also in 1966, they started to promote. Modern Engineering Technology Symposium. And he was in 1969. when he was the Secretary General of the Executive Yuan. Also participated in the development program. Of revitalizing the electronics industry. Li Kwok Din has been in business since 1976. After serving as a member of the Council for Science and Technology. I have been actively promoting the development of high technology. Including the establishment of Hsinchu Industrial Park. Introduction of Venture Capital Industry, etc. It's like it's driving 1985. H&Q established a subsidiary in Taiwan. With the 1980s. Taiwan's booming technology industry. This member of the Council for Science and Technology. He has become the driving force behind the technology industry. It is also a must-see for foreign visitors to Taiwan. That's why he is known as the “Godfather of Taiwan Technology”. So it is said that. Founding of TSMC in 1987. It was because of the development of the country at that time. The framework for development was already in place. The building department was also set up more than 20 years ago. Started to promote the long-term development. Of the electronics industry. And then there was Y.S. Sun. Worries of Li Kwok-Ting and others. What will be the emerging industries in Taiwan in 20 years? Everything works together. A product of the world. If it's copied today. Basically, it's pretty hard. But how did he catch up with the British and the Japanese? And even changed the whole world? As mentioned earlier. Now we have the technology, the equipment, and the plant. It's already started. The first step in the production of an integrated circuit. But to really create a huge change. But to really make a big difference,. It's going to take a lot of technology. really out there in the industry. In order to achieve this vision. Funding from the Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). Using a Derivative Company. Established Taiwan's first semiconductor manufacturing company. That's what we're talking about. The first graduate representative in the program. “UMC. The Republic of China at this time. Not only can they produce integrated circuits. The ability to design integrated circuits is now available.

But overall. We're still not as good as our teacher father. AKA America's Big Brother. So the government started betting money. Promoting the Development of Ultra-Large-Scale Integrated Circuits. Establishment of a 6” Wafer Production Laboratory. Ultra Large-Scale Integrated Circuits. It's about taking a very large number of transistors. Into a single chip. With transistors in between. Communicating and working together. If we are able to develop a successful business. It will shorten our relationship with the United States. And the technology gap with Japan. It can also indirectly lead to the growth of related applications. Of course, this program. And it successfully built the foundation of TSMC. And then a man came out of nowhere. He was the future leader of TSMC. Mr. Chang. Brother Zhang in August 1985. Came to Taiwan from the U.S. to take over the presidency of ITRI. Two weeks after his appointment. The then Administrative Officer in charge of Science and Technology. Li Kwok Din was asked to plan and set up a company. manufacturing company for integrated circuits. So it's official. Setting up TSMC's program. But in fact. Chang has never lived in Taiwan before. He was born in Ningbo in 1931. After being displaced by the war in the occupied territories. From Hong Kong to the U.S. in 1949. Also served as Vice President of Texas Instruments. Led the establishment of TI's Semiconductor Division. Very high reputation in the semiconductor industry. Late 1960s. He dominated Texas Instruments. Set up a packaging plant in Taiwan. Recognizing the excellent manufacturing environment in Taiwan. At the same time. We are beginning to actively promote the development. of high-tech industries. So this one at the time. It must be Zhang Zhongmou, who was listed as an SSR card. Naturally, he continues to be actively sought after. Expect him to help us build the semiconductor industry. When Y.S. Sun was the Executive Yuan Chief Executive Officer. And Li Kuo-ding's trip to the U.S. A visit to Texas Instruments. Have made invitations. But Zhang was still in a very high position at the time. He declined the offer for the time being. It was not until later that Zhang felt that. If he stays in Derby. It's probably not going to be easy to take it any further. So I left. After a short stint in charge of general purpose equipment, he left. Temporary stay at home with nothing to do. Mr. Hsu Hsien-hsiu, Chairman of ITRI. He made three trips to Moutain. It was only in 1985 that Chang accepted the invitation. He decided to leave the United States,. Where he had lived for more than 30 years. Came to Taiwan. Appointed Director of the Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). And the first thing he did. I decided to turn the lab into a factory. into a specialized foundry. 1987. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Also known as TSMC. Formally established in Hsinchu Industrial Science Park. Started to focus on “Foundry” manufacturing model. It's because Chang thinks that. Taiwan's IC design and technology development. At that time was a very important part of the economy. and marketing were relatively backward.

But with excellent manufacturing advantages. Excellent engineers, operators, etc. This led to his famous. Specialty Foundry Program. Just don't do the design. Instead, we specialize in OEM for our customers. Unlike the fabs of the time. Vertical integration of design and manufacturing. This type of production focuses on. Taiwan's own manufacturing expertise. It also meets the needs of many of our customers. Gradually. Taiwan's foundry industry is booming. After the success. Taking the world a step further. Including Taiwan's IC design company trend. It can be said that TSMC is a company. It's changed the world. How the Semiconductor Industry Works. The Next 30 Years. The advanced countries. Each specializes in its own strengths. U.S.-specific product design. Taiwan specializes in wafer production and also R&D wafers. Other countries sell their products. But you may not know this. In fact, our TSMC. It's a group of loyal and patriotic scientists. It's all in the hands of a group of loyal and patriotic men and women. In order to gain relevant competitiveness. Many semiconductor companies in Taiwan. All of them aim to lead the world in product technology. Concentration. Continuous Innovation. In the case of TSMC. Back in 1998. The world's first 0.18 micron. Low Power Process Technology. After that, we were ahead of our competitors every two years. Introducing Next Generation Low Power Process Technology. These technologies have a wide range of applications. Tablets, cell phones, Bluetooth. to all kinds of portable electronics. You can see its application in all kinds of portable electronics. In addition recently developed by TSMC. 1.6 Nano Nanotechnology. It's the only one of its kind in the world. The ability to always innovate. Building Competitive Strength. It's taking the world out of the Nano era. And it's pulling the world into the Age of Eternity. But has it ever occurred to you that. Why did Chang say. Taiwan has good engineers and operators. From the very beginning. It's a continuation of the foundation of the continental period. In the Republic of China. Chiao Tung University, formerly known as Nanyang Public School. The most important engineering school in China. The future talents are widely distributed in both sides. Of the Taiwan Strait and overseas. In particular, there is a group of talents. They went to America to escape the war. After the Republic of China moved to Taiwan. A group of students from Jiaotong University came to Taiwan. Taking over the early stages of the construction of Taiwan. Capital construction. And 1966. These U.S.-based engineering experts came to Taiwan to hold the. The First Modern Engineering Technology Symposium. In fact, it's just a way to teach new knowledge in Taiwan. The reason why they. The reason why they are actively. Helping us to promote the electronics industry. In fact, it also comes from. Their patriotism for China. Although they are in the United States. But because of their upbringing.

It happened to be at a time when China. Was facing a lot of internal and external problems. So I'm willing to help. We, the national government, are striving for industrialization. And of course,. We had this great group of scientists and technicians. It's mainly because of the beginning of the Qing Dynasty. Chinese intellectuals believed that. to participate in the trend of industrialization to save the country. They think it's a responsibility. It could also be a practical way out. So there's a high percentage of intellectuals. Chose to study engineering. And 1949. After the government moved to Taiwan. Immediate Promotion of Universal Education. And Polytechnic Education. Later, Jiao Tong University and Tsinghua University. also resumed in Taiwan. Both schools emphasized science and technology at that time. Jiao Tong University also immediately set up. An electronics research institute. Between 1950 and 1970. Institutions of higher education have accumulated. produced more than 30,000 engineers. For Industrialization. Especially the development of Taiwan's technology. Industry in the future. Prepare for the abundant human resources. But someone asked. TSMC is doing so well now. It's got to be Papa America. But is that really what happened? Of course, every time we talk about. Taiwan's semiconductor industry. A bunch of disaster victims would come and say. TSMC's U.S. shareholding is the largest. It should be called USMC. But Revan. Have you thought about your party? Include TSMC in all of China. Is TSMC the first of the top 500 companies in China? But since there is such a thing as. I think it comes from the same place. So let's get down to business. It's the beginning. TSMC announced in April 2024 that. Nominees for 10 new directors. and will be held on June. The re-election of Directors will be held. At the Annual General Meeting of Shareholders on June. A message was then circulated on the internet. It was claimed that for a long time. TSMC's board ratio is. 76% foreign-invested, 24% Taiwanese-invested. Including TSMC's President, Mr. Chieh-Chia Wei. All nine directors are foreign nationals. Claims that TSMC is controlled by foreign investors. Let's start with the fact that the directors are all foreigners. Let's start by analyzing this case. TSMC's website and annual report. There is also public information set up. By the stock exchange to show that. Currently, TSMC's Board of Directors consists of 10 members. Of which 1 is a director of government stock. Also known as the National Development Fund. Of the Executive Yuan. And 6 independent directors. TSMC's Board of Directors. There are 4 people with ROC citizenship. 5 people of foreign nationality. 1 person with both ROC and foreign nationality. That's why there's a rumor going around. The nine directors are all foreigners. This is obviously not true. I should also mention that. Our TSMC. It's a company with separate ownership and operation.

The directors are there because of their expertise. so they are elected by the shareholders. It has nothing to do with whether or not you hold shares. Even if some members of the board of directors have shares. But the percentage is still quite low. There's no way to control a company with a single shareholding. Because TSMC is not listed overseas. So foreign investors. want to own TSMC stock. you will have to go through certain organizations. to purchase depositary receipts. And the percentage of shares held by these organizations. In fact, it represents a lot of foreign investors. It's not a single shareholder. So the members of TSMC's board of directors. Although there are foreigners. But no one is a foreign investor. And no foreign director owns TSMC stock. Let's take a look at this chart. So the rumor doesn't hold water. So that's the composition of the board of directors. Let's talk about the distribution of shares. Based on publicly available information. TSMC Shareholder Structure. We can tell. Current foreign ownership of TSMC shares. Approximately 75.86. The rest of the shareholding. Is dispersed amongst governmental organizations. Financial Institutions, Various Legal Entities. And in the hands of our average retail investor. But foreign investors own 70% of TSMC's stock. Can we control the whole TSMC? The answer is probably still no. It's because these foreign investors are very diversified. Including European and American countries. Especially American investment institutions. Mutual Funds and Individuals. But in fact, it also includes Hong Kong. Singapore Government, Norwegian Central Bank. And even organizations around the world such as the Fallen Zone. And then there's the sum of individual investor holdings. These 70% of the shares are distributed globally. More than 7,000 foreign corporations and funds. Behind each of these organizations,. There are many foreign investors. And so. Even if foreign ownership is high. However, they are not uniformly concentrated in a single stock. or in the hands of a few. After all, who can do anything? What about the idea of unifying such a large group of people? Uh...no. I've forgotten about His Holiness. And the idea of unification doesn't matter. This man needs to be able to unite the ranks. Significant impact on TSMC with the implementation of the holding. Basically, it's still not going to happen. Same situation. In fact, Samsung in Korea. Or Alibaba before the foreign investors pulled out. It's a similar situation. Currently Samsung's largest shareholding is also in the US. But Samsung has not become American. Because the largest shareholding. Same as “Investment Institutions. And there are countless other clients behind it. High Foreign Ownership. Compare to the world's consensus. You're going to do well as a business. I'd like to invest in you. But back to the point. The sadder part is that. The money we make at TSMC. It's still a big part of it. Because of the dividend. It's just that the foreigners are making money out of it. But if you really want to be strict about the math.

Single Legal Entity. TSMC has the largest percentage of shareholding. In addition to TSMC itself. I think it's still our Executive Yuan. The National Development Fund Management Board. Is now at 6.38%.

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DgKowmSA17Q

今天的故事

就是要來介紹台積電

是怎麼從一個

默默無聞的小企業

逐漸成為全球半導體的領先企業

進而有了「護國神山」的稱號

除了因為它有世界級的成就以外

也因為大家都認定

它對這個世界

是不可或缺的存在

有助於保護我們中華民國的現狀

所以今天我們就來聊聊

這座護國神山！

還有網路上傳了很久的

台積電就是美積電

但事實真的是這樣嗎？

#臺灣 #台灣 #中華民國 #中國 #歷史 #tsmc #台積電 #半導體 #張忠謀 #護國神山 #美積電 #美國 #奈米 #晶片 #埃 #三星 #工研院 #行政院 #理工男 #新竹科學園區 #竹科 #國產

📍讓我們繼續活下去 ► http://bit.ly/2tGL3eB

LINE貼圖搜尋「我的學習筆記」或是點下方連結

我的學習筆記2

https://reurl.cc/qkk38y

我的學習筆記3

https://reurl.cc/n77Rn6

我的學習筆記4

https://reurl.cc/mllNq9

🎬更多的學習筆記

YOUTUBE長影片討論 ► https://reurl.cc/gy2ZR

Tiktok上更多短影片 ► https://reurl.cc/bkjjz3

Facebook關注更多消息 ► https://bit.ly/2TFDZXC

Instagram掌握國際大事 ► https://reurl.cc/GyxEp

方格子學術公眾化成果 ► http://bit.ly/32J6mrK

DisCord群組一起聊天抬槓►https://discord.gg/YmSkbSVrUC

小額支付贊助我歐付寶►http://bit.ly/2Z1s1yA

當然還有PayPal►PayPal.Me/wMyNotebook

😉特別感謝5月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
item.description:
Hello! Hello, everyone! I'm a niu ge. Whenever people talk about Korea. The first company that comes to mind. Probably either Samsung or LG. And whenever we talk about Japan. SONY might come to mind. Or Yamaha or something like that. When it comes to us in Taiwan. Which company comes to mind? There should be 87% of them. I think it's TSMC. That's right. So today's story. It's about TSMC. How did he go from a. small, unheard-of business. Becoming a global leader in semiconductors. The name “Protector of the Nation” was given to the company. In addition to its world-class achievements. It's also because it's recognized. It is to the world. It's an indispensable presence. Helps protect the status quo in the Republic of China. Seems to be recognized as representing the Republic of China. Achievements in political democratization and social modernization. and a free market economy. Outstanding Representatives of the Private Sector. And what about that Yulon you mentioned? Uh...I'm afraid of getting sued. So let's talk today. The mountain that protects the nation. And the one that's been circulating on the Internet for a long time. TSMC is MSMC. But is that really the case? Let's start with the founding of TSMC. TSMC's Founding. Roughly February 1987, before the end of martial law. It's the same as the foundation. From Taiwan's pre-war industrial policy. Leaders of the Construction Industry at the Time. and the Chinese in China and abroad. It's based on something like an ode to the Republic of China. An ode to Chinese nationalism. Wanting to promote industry to save the country. At the same time it can be said to be a. A true public sector project. A Public Sector Initiative. But when we first. In order to avoid the rigidity of the state-run business norms. That's why the official government shareholding is less than half. It also has a significant impact on TSMC's future operations. There's not a lot of interference. The only thing I can say is that it's good that it's not state-run. Otherwise, we wouldn't have the Protectorate today. TSMC was founded in 1987. Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). It's also known as the Industrial Technology Research Institute. A spin-off of ITRI's Electronics Research Institute. But TSMC was actually. Promoted by the Executive Yuan at the beginning. Phase II of the Semiconductor Long-Term Program. Graduates of Phase I. UMC is also quite famous. Also known as UMC. It was famous in 1980. And ITRI, which is committed to the program. was established in 1973. In other words. Before TSMC was founded. In fact, the relevant departments in Taiwan. have been around for more than 20 years. It's been paving the way. A Program to Promote the Development. Of Taiwan's Semiconductor Industry. A typical forward-looking industrial policy. During the time of the Republic of China's relocation to Taiwan. Taiwan is still a.

A typical colonial economy dependent on rice and sugar exports. Rice and sugar accounted for over 70% of exports. Luckily. After the war through a successful industrial policy. By the end of the 1960s. Taiwan's economy had successfully undergone. Initial industrialization. By 1970. Industrial goods already accounted for nearly 80% of exports. The share of rice and sugar has dropped to 3%. In the early stages of industrialization. It is not difficult to choose the target industry. You can refer to the development history of advanced countries. Firstly, the textile industry was the main light industry. For the people's livelihood. And then the upstream steel and petrochemical industries. But as we move forward. have caught up with the advanced countries. What's next? The officials in charge at the time. They all had a sense of worry. Worrying from time to time. What will be the new industries in Taiwan in 20 years? That's why the ministers of economic affairs at that time. Li Kuo-ting and Sun Yun-suan. Responsible for leading the Economic. And Construction Department. Thinking about where Taiwan's next new industry will be. And at that time, the U.S. was in the midst of a rapidly developing. The Emerging Electronic Information Industry. Looks like a promising target. The organization promoting industrial policy at the time. And the system was pretty much in place. The top political echelons are also keeping an eye. On the development. But in the process of promoting. Almost everything. has met with considerable resistance. Including the establishment. Of the Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). First proposal to promote the IC industry. Years later, the establishment of TSMC was proposed. Plan to operate a specialized foundry. All of which have been met with strong opposition. Like spending so much money on an investment. Will there be a return or something like that? But Sun is Sun. He's got it all figured out. It took more than a year. It convinced the old countrymen at the time. Getting the Legislature to agree. Finally succeeded in getting the consortium to agree to. Establishment of Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). Simultaneous. Mr. Sun and others have also been actively promoting. The Development of Taiwan's Electronics Industry. But such a new technology. You can't figure it out on your own. So Sun and the best of the best in the United States. Poon Wen-yuan jointly promoted the “Integrated Circuit Project”. He also convened the meeting with Mr. Chin-Tai Shih,. Mr. Ting-Yuan Yang, Mr. Ching-Chu Chang, and others. A group of young scholars joined the program. With Radio Corporation of America (RCA). Technology Transfer Cooperation. In a team of 19 people. We belong to design, manufacturing. Testing, equipment and other 4 groups. They came all the way to the United States. RCA Headquarters “Across the Sea”. A Practical Look at Semiconductor Manufacturing. And how a factory works. The team at the time. In addition to the short period of time. I'm just trying to absorb everything. It is also necessary to regularly report progress to the mainland. Purchase and build a plant in Taiwan. Process Equipment Required.

Yes, sir! You heard right. And while they're at it. ITRI was already working on it. Building Taiwan's First Wafer Demonstration Plant. Waiting for everyone to come back. We're ready to go. It's really fierce. It's not like some officials. I came back from a trip abroad with three big words. I'm impressed. 1977. ITRI's Institute of Electronics has set up. Integrated Circuit Demonstration Factory. 7.5 micron process. As a Primary Target. In December of that year. The yield rate was very good. Even exceeded RCA's estimate. And soon after. The factory successfully received the order. And so it begins. The era of self-produced integrated circuits. And many others. They're also actively contributing to the development. For example, Ronny Fei was the Undersecretary of Transportation. Also in 1966, they started to promote. Modern Engineering Technology Symposium. And he was in 1969. when he was the Secretary General of the Executive Yuan. Also participated in the development program. Of revitalizing the electronics industry. Li Kwok Din has been in business since 1976. After serving as a member of the Council for Science and Technology. I have been actively promoting the development of high technology. Including the establishment of Hsinchu Industrial Park. Introduction of Venture Capital Industry, etc. It's like it's driving 1985. H&Q established a subsidiary in Taiwan. With the 1980s. Taiwan's booming technology industry. This member of the Council for Science and Technology. He has become the driving force behind the technology industry. It is also a must-see for foreign visitors to Taiwan. That's why he is known as the “Godfather of Taiwan Technology”. So it is said that. Founding of TSMC in 1987. It was because of the development of the country at that time. The framework for development was already in place. The building department was also set up more than 20 years ago. Started to promote the long-term development. Of the electronics industry. And then there was Y.S. Sun. Worries of Li Kwok-Ting and others. What will be the emerging industries in Taiwan in 20 years? Everything works together. A product of the world. If it's copied today. Basically, it's pretty hard. But how did he catch up with the British and the Japanese? And even changed the whole world? As mentioned earlier. Now we have the technology, the equipment, and the plant. It's already started. The first step in the production of an integrated circuit. But to really create a huge change. But to really make a big difference,. It's going to take a lot of technology. really out there in the industry. In order to achieve this vision. Funding from the Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). Using a Derivative Company. Established Taiwan's first semiconductor manufacturing company. That's what we're talking about. The first graduate representative in the program. “UMC. The Republic of China at this time. Not only can they produce integrated circuits. The ability to design integrated circuits is now available.

But overall. We're still not as good as our teacher father. AKA America's Big Brother. So the government started betting money. Promoting the Development of Ultra-Large-Scale Integrated Circuits. Establishment of a 6” Wafer Production Laboratory. Ultra Large-Scale Integrated Circuits. It's about taking a very large number of transistors. Into a single chip. With transistors in between. Communicating and working together. If we are able to develop a successful business. It will shorten our relationship with the United States. And the technology gap with Japan. It can also indirectly lead to the growth of related applications. Of course, this program. And it successfully built the foundation of TSMC. And then a man came out of nowhere. He was the future leader of TSMC. Mr. Chang. Brother Zhang in August 1985. Came to Taiwan from the U.S. to take over the presidency of ITRI. Two weeks after his appointment. The then Administrative Officer in charge of Science and Technology. Li Kwok Din was asked to plan and set up a company. manufacturing company for integrated circuits. So it's official. Setting up TSMC's program. But in fact. Chang has never lived in Taiwan before. He was born in Ningbo in 1931. After being displaced by the war in the occupied territories. From Hong Kong to the U.S. in 1949. Also served as Vice President of Texas Instruments. Led the establishment of TI's Semiconductor Division. Very high reputation in the semiconductor industry. Late 1960s. He dominated Texas Instruments. Set up a packaging plant in Taiwan. Recognizing the excellent manufacturing environment in Taiwan. At the same time. We are beginning to actively promote the development. of high-tech industries. So this one at the time. It must be Zhang Zhongmou, who was listed as an SSR card. Naturally, he continues to be actively sought after. Expect him to help us build the semiconductor industry. When Y.S. Sun was the Executive Yuan Chief Executive Officer. And Li Kuo-ding's trip to the U.S. A visit to Texas Instruments. Have made invitations. But Zhang was still in a very high position at the time. He declined the offer for the time being. It was not until later that Zhang felt that. If he stays in Derby. It's probably not going to be easy to take it any further. So I left. After a short stint in charge of general purpose equipment, he left. Temporary stay at home with nothing to do. Mr. Hsu Hsien-hsiu, Chairman of ITRI. He made three trips to Moutain. It was only in 1985 that Chang accepted the invitation. He decided to leave the United States,. Where he had lived for more than 30 years. Came to Taiwan. Appointed Director of the Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). And the first thing he did. I decided to turn the lab into a factory. into a specialized foundry. 1987. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Also known as TSMC. Formally established in Hsinchu Industrial Science Park. Started to focus on “Foundry” manufacturing model. It's because Chang thinks that. Taiwan's IC design and technology development. At that time was a very important part of the economy. and marketing were relatively backward.

But with excellent manufacturing advantages. Excellent engineers, operators, etc. This led to his famous. Specialty Foundry Program. Just don't do the design. Instead, we specialize in OEM for our customers. Unlike the fabs of the time. Vertical integration of design and manufacturing. This type of production focuses on. Taiwan's own manufacturing expertise. It also meets the needs of many of our customers. Gradually. Taiwan's foundry industry is booming. After the success. Taking the world a step further. Including Taiwan's IC design company trend. It can be said that TSMC is a company. It's changed the world. How the Semiconductor Industry Works. The Next 30 Years. The advanced countries. Each specializes in its own strengths. U.S.-specific product design. Taiwan specializes in wafer production and also R&D wafers. Other countries sell their products. But you may not know this. In fact, our TSMC. It's a group of loyal and patriotic scientists. It's all in the hands of a group of loyal and patriotic men and women. In order to gain relevant competitiveness. Many semiconductor companies in Taiwan. All of them aim to lead the world in product technology. Concentration. Continuous Innovation. In the case of TSMC. Back in 1998. The world's first 0.18 micron. Low Power Process Technology. After that, we were ahead of our competitors every two years. Introducing Next Generation Low Power Process Technology. These technologies have a wide range of applications. Tablets, cell phones, Bluetooth. to all kinds of portable electronics. You can see its application in all kinds of portable electronics. In addition recently developed by TSMC. 1.6 Nano Nanotechnology. It's the only one of its kind in the world. The ability to always innovate. Building Competitive Strength. It's taking the world out of the Nano era. And it's pulling the world into the Age of Eternity. But has it ever occurred to you that. Why did Chang say. Taiwan has good engineers and operators. From the very beginning. It's a continuation of the foundation of the continental period. In the Republic of China. Chiao Tung University, formerly known as Nanyang Public School. The most important engineering school in China. The future talents are widely distributed in both sides. Of the Taiwan Strait and overseas. In particular, there is a group of talents. They went to America to escape the war. After the Republic of China moved to Taiwan. A group of students from Jiaotong University came to Taiwan. Taking over the early stages of the construction of Taiwan. Capital construction. And 1966. These U.S.-based engineering experts came to Taiwan to hold the. The First Modern Engineering Technology Symposium. In fact, it's just a way to teach new knowledge in Taiwan. The reason why they. The reason why they are actively. Helping us to promote the electronics industry. In fact, it also comes from. Their patriotism for China. Although they are in the United States. But because of their upbringing.

It happened to be at a time when China. Was facing a lot of internal and external problems. So I'm willing to help. We, the national government, are striving for industrialization. And of course,. We had this great group of scientists and technicians. It's mainly because of the beginning of the Qing Dynasty. Chinese intellectuals believed that. to participate in the trend of industrialization to save the country. They think it's a responsibility. It could also be a practical way out. So there's a high percentage of intellectuals. Chose to study engineering. And 1949. After the government moved to Taiwan. Immediate Promotion of Universal Education. And Polytechnic Education. Later, Jiao Tong University and Tsinghua University. also resumed in Taiwan. Both schools emphasized science and technology at that time. Jiao Tong University also immediately set up. An electronics research institute. Between 1950 and 1970. Institutions of higher education have accumulated. produced more than 30,000 engineers. For Industrialization. Especially the development of Taiwan's technology. Industry in the future. Prepare for the abundant human resources. But someone asked. TSMC is doing so well now. It's got to be Papa America. But is that really what happened? Of course, every time we talk about. Taiwan's semiconductor industry. A bunch of disaster victims would come and say. TSMC's U.S. shareholding is the largest. It should be called USMC. But Revan. Have you thought about your party? Include TSMC in all of China. Is TSMC the first of the top 500 companies in China? But since there is such a thing as. I think it comes from the same place. So let's get down to business. It's the beginning. TSMC announced in April 2024 that. Nominees for 10 new directors. and will be held on June. The re-election of Directors will be held. At the Annual General Meeting of Shareholders on June. A message was then circulated on the internet. It was claimed that for a long time. TSMC's board ratio is. 76% foreign-invested, 24% Taiwanese-invested. Including TSMC's President, Mr. Chieh-Chia Wei. All nine directors are foreign nationals. Claims that TSMC is controlled by foreign investors. Let's start with the fact that the directors are all foreigners. Let's start by analyzing this case. TSMC's website and annual report. There is also public information set up. By the stock exchange to show that. Currently, TSMC's Board of Directors consists of 10 members. Of which 1 is a director of government stock. Also known as the National Development Fund. Of the Executive Yuan. And 6 independent directors. TSMC's Board of Directors. There are 4 people with ROC citizenship. 5 people of foreign nationality. 1 person with both ROC and foreign nationality. That's why there's a rumor going around. The nine directors are all foreigners. This is obviously not true. I should also mention that. Our TSMC. It's a company with separate ownership and operation.

The directors are there because of their expertise. so they are elected by the shareholders. It has nothing to do with whether or not you hold shares. Even if some members of the board of directors have shares. But the percentage is still quite low. There's no way to control a company with a single shareholding. Because TSMC is not listed overseas. So foreign investors. want to own TSMC stock. you will have to go through certain organizations. to purchase depositary receipts. And the percentage of shares held by these organizations. In fact, it represents a lot of foreign investors. It's not a single shareholder. So the members of TSMC's board of directors. Although there are foreigners. But no one is a foreign investor. And no foreign director owns TSMC stock. Let's take a look at this chart. So the rumor doesn't hold water. So that's the composition of the board of directors. Let's talk about the distribution of shares. Based on publicly available information. TSMC Shareholder Structure. We can tell. Current foreign ownership of TSMC shares. Approximately 75.86. The rest of the shareholding. Is dispersed amongst governmental organizations. Financial Institutions, Various Legal Entities. And in the hands of our average retail investor. But foreign investors own 70% of TSMC's stock. Can we control the whole TSMC? The answer is probably still no. It's because these foreign investors are very diversified. Including European and American countries. Especially American investment institutions. Mutual Funds and Individuals. But in fact, it also includes Hong Kong. Singapore Government, Norwegian Central Bank. And even organizations around the world such as the Fallen Zone. And then there's the sum of individual investor holdings. These 70% of the shares are distributed globally. More than 7,000 foreign corporations and funds. Behind each of these organizations,. There are many foreign investors. And so. Even if foreign ownership is high. However, they are not uniformly concentrated in a single stock. or in the hands of a few. After all, who can do anything? What about the idea of unifying such a large group of people? Uh...no. I've forgotten about His Holiness. And the idea of unification doesn't matter. This man needs to be able to unite the ranks. Significant impact on TSMC with the implementation of the holding. Basically, it's still not going to happen. Same situation. In fact, Samsung in Korea. Or Alibaba before the foreign investors pulled out. It's a similar situation. Currently Samsung's largest shareholding is also in the US. But Samsung has not become American. Because the largest shareholding. Same as “Investment Institutions. And there are countless other clients behind it. High Foreign Ownership. Compare to the world's consensus. You're going to do well as a business. I'd like to invest in you. But back to the point. The sadder part is that. The money we make at TSMC. It's still a big part of it. Because of the dividend. It's just that the foreigners are making money out of it. But if you really want to be strict about the math.

Single Legal Entity. TSMC has the largest percentage of shareholding. In addition to TSMC itself. I think it's still our Executive Yuan. The National Development Fund Management Board. Is now at 6.38%.

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DgKowmSA17Q

今天的故事

就是要來介紹台積電

是怎麼從一個

默默無聞的小企業

逐漸成為全球半導體的領先企業

進而有了「護國神山」的稱號

除了因為它有世界級的成就以外

也因為大家都認定

它對這個世界

是不可或缺的存在

有助於保護我們中華民國的現狀

所以今天我們就來聊聊

這座護國神山！

還有網路上傳了很久的

台積電就是美積電

但事實真的是這樣嗎？

#臺灣 #台灣 #中華民國 #中國 #歷史 #tsmc #台積電 #半導體 #張忠謀 #護國神山 #美積電 #美國 #奈米 #晶片 #埃 #三星 #工研院 #行政院 #理工男 #新竹科學園區 #竹科 #國產

📍讓我們繼續活下去 ► http://bit.ly/2tGL3eB

LINE貼圖搜尋「我的學習筆記」或是點下方連結

我的學習筆記2

https://reurl.cc/qkk38y

我的學習筆記3

https://reurl.cc/n77Rn6

我的學習筆記4

https://reurl.cc/mllNq9

🎬更多的學習筆記

YOUTUBE長影片討論 ► https://reurl.cc/gy2ZR

Tiktok上更多短影片 ► https://reurl.cc/bkjjz3

Facebook關注更多消息 ► https://bit.ly/2TFDZXC

Instagram掌握國際大事 ► https://reurl.cc/GyxEp

方格子學術公眾化成果 ► http://bit.ly/32J6mrK

DisCord群組一起聊天抬槓►https://discord.gg/YmSkbSVrUC

小額支付贊助我歐付寶►http://bit.ly/2Z1s1yA

當然還有PayPal►PayPal.Me/wMyNotebook

😉特別感謝5月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
item.content:encoded:
Hello! Hello, everyone! I'm a niu ge. Whenever people talk about Korea. The first company that comes to mind. Probably either Samsung or LG. And whenever we talk about Japan. SONY might come to mind. Or Yamaha or something like that. When it comes to us in Taiwan. Which company comes to mind? There should be 87% of them. I think it's TSMC. That's right. So today's story. It's about TSMC. How did he go from a. small, unheard-of business. Becoming a global leader in semiconductors. The name “Protector of the Nation” was given to the company. In addition to its world-class achievements. It's also because it's recognized. It is to the world. It's an indispensable presence. Helps protect the status quo in the Republic of China. Seems to be recognized as representing the Republic of China. Achievements in political democratization and social modernization. and a free market economy. Outstanding Representatives of the Private Sector. And what about that Yulon you mentioned? Uh...I'm afraid of getting sued. So let's talk today. The mountain that protects the nation. And the one that's been circulating on the Internet for a long time. TSMC is MSMC. But is that really the case? Let's start with the founding of TSMC. TSMC's Founding. Roughly February 1987, before the end of martial law. It's the same as the foundation. From Taiwan's pre-war industrial policy. Leaders of the Construction Industry at the Time. and the Chinese in China and abroad. It's based on something like an ode to the Republic of China. An ode to Chinese nationalism. Wanting to promote industry to save the country. At the same time it can be said to be a. A true public sector project. A Public Sector Initiative. But when we first. In order to avoid the rigidity of the state-run business norms. That's why the official government shareholding is less than half. It also has a significant impact on TSMC's future operations. There's not a lot of interference. The only thing I can say is that it's good that it's not state-run. Otherwise, we wouldn't have the Protectorate today. TSMC was founded in 1987. Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). It's also known as the Industrial Technology Research Institute. A spin-off of ITRI's Electronics Research Institute. But TSMC was actually. Promoted by the Executive Yuan at the beginning. Phase II of the Semiconductor Long-Term Program. Graduates of Phase I. UMC is also quite famous. Also known as UMC. It was famous in 1980. And ITRI, which is committed to the program. was established in 1973. In other words. Before TSMC was founded. In fact, the relevant departments in Taiwan. have been around for more than 20 years. It's been paving the way. A Program to Promote the Development. Of Taiwan's Semiconductor Industry. A typical forward-looking industrial policy. During the time of the Republic of China's relocation to Taiwan. Taiwan is still a.

A typical colonial economy dependent on rice and sugar exports. Rice and sugar accounted for over 70% of exports. Luckily. After the war through a successful industrial policy. By the end of the 1960s. Taiwan's economy had successfully undergone. Initial industrialization. By 1970. Industrial goods already accounted for nearly 80% of exports. The share of rice and sugar has dropped to 3%. In the early stages of industrialization. It is not difficult to choose the target industry. You can refer to the development history of advanced countries. Firstly, the textile industry was the main light industry. For the people's livelihood. And then the upstream steel and petrochemical industries. But as we move forward. have caught up with the advanced countries. What's next? The officials in charge at the time. They all had a sense of worry. Worrying from time to time. What will be the new industries in Taiwan in 20 years? That's why the ministers of economic affairs at that time. Li Kuo-ting and Sun Yun-suan. Responsible for leading the Economic. And Construction Department. Thinking about where Taiwan's next new industry will be. And at that time, the U.S. was in the midst of a rapidly developing. The Emerging Electronic Information Industry. Looks like a promising target. The organization promoting industrial policy at the time. And the system was pretty much in place. The top political echelons are also keeping an eye. On the development. But in the process of promoting. Almost everything. has met with considerable resistance. Including the establishment. Of the Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). First proposal to promote the IC industry. Years later, the establishment of TSMC was proposed. Plan to operate a specialized foundry. All of which have been met with strong opposition. Like spending so much money on an investment. Will there be a return or something like that? But Sun is Sun. He's got it all figured out. It took more than a year. It convinced the old countrymen at the time. Getting the Legislature to agree. Finally succeeded in getting the consortium to agree to. Establishment of Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). Simultaneous. Mr. Sun and others have also been actively promoting. The Development of Taiwan's Electronics Industry. But such a new technology. You can't figure it out on your own. So Sun and the best of the best in the United States. Poon Wen-yuan jointly promoted the “Integrated Circuit Project”. He also convened the meeting with Mr. Chin-Tai Shih,. Mr. Ting-Yuan Yang, Mr. Ching-Chu Chang, and others. A group of young scholars joined the program. With Radio Corporation of America (RCA). Technology Transfer Cooperation. In a team of 19 people. We belong to design, manufacturing. Testing, equipment and other 4 groups. They came all the way to the United States. RCA Headquarters “Across the Sea”. A Practical Look at Semiconductor Manufacturing. And how a factory works. The team at the time. In addition to the short period of time. I'm just trying to absorb everything. It is also necessary to regularly report progress to the mainland. Purchase and build a plant in Taiwan. Process Equipment Required.

Yes, sir! You heard right. And while they're at it. ITRI was already working on it. Building Taiwan's First Wafer Demonstration Plant. Waiting for everyone to come back. We're ready to go. It's really fierce. It's not like some officials. I came back from a trip abroad with three big words. I'm impressed. 1977. ITRI's Institute of Electronics has set up. Integrated Circuit Demonstration Factory. 7.5 micron process. As a Primary Target. In December of that year. The yield rate was very good. Even exceeded RCA's estimate. And soon after. The factory successfully received the order. And so it begins. The era of self-produced integrated circuits. And many others. They're also actively contributing to the development. For example, Ronny Fei was the Undersecretary of Transportation. Also in 1966, they started to promote. Modern Engineering Technology Symposium. And he was in 1969. when he was the Secretary General of the Executive Yuan. Also participated in the development program. Of revitalizing the electronics industry. Li Kwok Din has been in business since 1976. After serving as a member of the Council for Science and Technology. I have been actively promoting the development of high technology. Including the establishment of Hsinchu Industrial Park. Introduction of Venture Capital Industry, etc. It's like it's driving 1985. H&Q established a subsidiary in Taiwan. With the 1980s. Taiwan's booming technology industry. This member of the Council for Science and Technology. He has become the driving force behind the technology industry. It is also a must-see for foreign visitors to Taiwan. That's why he is known as the “Godfather of Taiwan Technology”. So it is said that. Founding of TSMC in 1987. It was because of the development of the country at that time. The framework for development was already in place. The building department was also set up more than 20 years ago. Started to promote the long-term development. Of the electronics industry. And then there was Y.S. Sun. Worries of Li Kwok-Ting and others. What will be the emerging industries in Taiwan in 20 years? Everything works together. A product of the world. If it's copied today. Basically, it's pretty hard. But how did he catch up with the British and the Japanese? And even changed the whole world? As mentioned earlier. Now we have the technology, the equipment, and the plant. It's already started. The first step in the production of an integrated circuit. But to really create a huge change. But to really make a big difference,. It's going to take a lot of technology. really out there in the industry. In order to achieve this vision. Funding from the Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). Using a Derivative Company. Established Taiwan's first semiconductor manufacturing company. That's what we're talking about. The first graduate representative in the program. “UMC. The Republic of China at this time. Not only can they produce integrated circuits. The ability to design integrated circuits is now available.

But overall. We're still not as good as our teacher father. AKA America's Big Brother. So the government started betting money. Promoting the Development of Ultra-Large-Scale Integrated Circuits. Establishment of a 6” Wafer Production Laboratory. Ultra Large-Scale Integrated Circuits. It's about taking a very large number of transistors. Into a single chip. With transistors in between. Communicating and working together. If we are able to develop a successful business. It will shorten our relationship with the United States. And the technology gap with Japan. It can also indirectly lead to the growth of related applications. Of course, this program. And it successfully built the foundation of TSMC. And then a man came out of nowhere. He was the future leader of TSMC. Mr. Chang. Brother Zhang in August 1985. Came to Taiwan from the U.S. to take over the presidency of ITRI. Two weeks after his appointment. The then Administrative Officer in charge of Science and Technology. Li Kwok Din was asked to plan and set up a company. manufacturing company for integrated circuits. So it's official. Setting up TSMC's program. But in fact. Chang has never lived in Taiwan before. He was born in Ningbo in 1931. After being displaced by the war in the occupied territories. From Hong Kong to the U.S. in 1949. Also served as Vice President of Texas Instruments. Led the establishment of TI's Semiconductor Division. Very high reputation in the semiconductor industry. Late 1960s. He dominated Texas Instruments. Set up a packaging plant in Taiwan. Recognizing the excellent manufacturing environment in Taiwan. At the same time. We are beginning to actively promote the development. of high-tech industries. So this one at the time. It must be Zhang Zhongmou, who was listed as an SSR card. Naturally, he continues to be actively sought after. Expect him to help us build the semiconductor industry. When Y.S. Sun was the Executive Yuan Chief Executive Officer. And Li Kuo-ding's trip to the U.S. A visit to Texas Instruments. Have made invitations. But Zhang was still in a very high position at the time. He declined the offer for the time being. It was not until later that Zhang felt that. If he stays in Derby. It's probably not going to be easy to take it any further. So I left. After a short stint in charge of general purpose equipment, he left. Temporary stay at home with nothing to do. Mr. Hsu Hsien-hsiu, Chairman of ITRI. He made three trips to Moutain. It was only in 1985 that Chang accepted the invitation. He decided to leave the United States,. Where he had lived for more than 30 years. Came to Taiwan. Appointed Director of the Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI). And the first thing he did. I decided to turn the lab into a factory. into a specialized foundry. 1987. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Also known as TSMC. Formally established in Hsinchu Industrial Science Park. Started to focus on “Foundry” manufacturing model. It's because Chang thinks that. Taiwan's IC design and technology development. At that time was a very important part of the economy. and marketing were relatively backward.

But with excellent manufacturing advantages. Excellent engineers, operators, etc. This led to his famous. Specialty Foundry Program. Just don't do the design. Instead, we specialize in OEM for our customers. Unlike the fabs of the time. Vertical integration of design and manufacturing. This type of production focuses on. Taiwan's own manufacturing expertise. It also meets the needs of many of our customers. Gradually. Taiwan's foundry industry is booming. After the success. Taking the world a step further. Including Taiwan's IC design company trend. It can be said that TSMC is a company. It's changed the world. How the Semiconductor Industry Works. The Next 30 Years. The advanced countries. Each specializes in its own strengths. U.S.-specific product design. Taiwan specializes in wafer production and also R&D wafers. Other countries sell their products. But you may not know this. In fact, our TSMC. It's a group of loyal and patriotic scientists. It's all in the hands of a group of loyal and patriotic men and women. In order to gain relevant competitiveness. Many semiconductor companies in Taiwan. All of them aim to lead the world in product technology. Concentration. Continuous Innovation. In the case of TSMC. Back in 1998. The world's first 0.18 micron. Low Power Process Technology. After that, we were ahead of our competitors every two years. Introducing Next Generation Low Power Process Technology. These technologies have a wide range of applications. Tablets, cell phones, Bluetooth. to all kinds of portable electronics. You can see its application in all kinds of portable electronics. In addition recently developed by TSMC. 1.6 Nano Nanotechnology. It's the only one of its kind in the world. The ability to always innovate. Building Competitive Strength. It's taking the world out of the Nano era. And it's pulling the world into the Age of Eternity. But has it ever occurred to you that. Why did Chang say. Taiwan has good engineers and operators. From the very beginning. It's a continuation of the foundation of the continental period. In the Republic of China. Chiao Tung University, formerly known as Nanyang Public School. The most important engineering school in China. The future talents are widely distributed in both sides. Of the Taiwan Strait and overseas. In particular, there is a group of talents. They went to America to escape the war. After the Republic of China moved to Taiwan. A group of students from Jiaotong University came to Taiwan. The First Modern Engineering Technology Symposium. In fact, it's just a way to teach new knowledge in Taiwan. The reason why they. The reason why they are actively. Helping us to promote the electronics industry. In fact, it also comes from. Their patriotism for China. Although they are in the United States. But because of their upbringing.

It happened to be at a time when China. Was facing a lot of internal and external problems. So I'm willing to help. We, the national government, are striving for industrialization. And of course,. We had this great group of scientists and technicians. It's mainly because of the beginning of the Qing Dynasty. Chinese intellectuals believed that. to participate in the trend of industrialization to save the country. They think it's a responsibility. It could also be a practical way out. So there's a high percentage of intellectuals. Chose to study engineering. And 1949. After the government moved to Taiwan. Immediate Promotion of Universal Education. And Polytechnic Education. Later, Jiao Tong University and Tsinghua University. also resumed in Taiwan. Both schools emphasized science and technology at that time. Jiao Tong University also immediately set up. An electronics research institute. Between 1950 and 1970. Institutions of higher education have accumulated. produced more than 30,000 engineers. For Industrialization. Especially the development of Taiwan's technology. Industry in the future. Prepare for the abundant human resources. But someone asked. TSMC is doing so well now. It's got to be Papa America. But is that really what happened? Of course, every time we talk about. Taiwan's semiconductor industry. A bunch of disaster victims would come and say. TSMC's U.S. shareholding is the largest. It should be called USMC. But Revan. Have you thought about your party? Include TSMC in all of China. Is TSMC the first of the top 500 companies in China? But since there is such a thing as. I think it comes from the same place. So let's get down to business. It's the beginning. TSMC announced in April 2024 that. Nominees for 10 new directors. and will be held on June. The re-election of Directors will be held. At the Annual General Meeting of Shareholders on June. A message was then circulated on the internet. It was claimed that for a long time. TSMC's board ratio is. 76% foreign-invested, 24% Taiwanese-invested. Including TSMC's President, Mr. Chieh-Chia Wei. All nine directors are foreign nationals. Claims that TSMC is controlled by foreign investors. Let's start with the fact that the directors are all foreigners. Let's start by analyzing this case. TSMC's website and annual report. There is also public information set up. By the stock exchange to show that. Currently, TSMC's Board of Directors consists of 10 members. Of which 1 is a director of government stock. Also known as the National Development Fund. Of the Executive Yuan. And 6 independent directors. TSMC's Board of Directors. There are 4 people with ROC citizenship. 5 people of foreign nationality. 1 person with both ROC and foreign nationality. That's why there's a rumor going around. The nine directors are all foreigners. This is obviously not true. I should also mention that. Our TSMC. It's a company with separate ownership and operation.

The directors are there because of their expertise. so they are elected by the shareholders. It has nothing to do with whether or not you hold shares. Even if some members of the board of directors have shares. But the percentage is still quite low. There's no way to control a company with a single shareholding. Because TSMC is not listed overseas. So foreign investors. want to own TSMC stock. you will have to go through certain organizations. to purchase depositary receipts. And the percentage of shares held by these organizations. In fact, it represents a lot of foreign investors. It's not a single shareholder. So the members of TSMC's board of directors. Although there are foreigners. But no one is a foreign investor. And no foreign director owns TSMC stock. Let's take a look at this chart. So the rumor doesn't hold water. So that's the composition of the board of directors. Let's talk about the distribution of shares. Based on publicly available information. TSMC Shareholder Structure. We can tell. Current foreign ownership of TSMC shares. Approximately 75.86. The rest of the shareholding. Is dispersed amongst governmental organizations. Financial Institutions, Various Legal Entities. And in the hands of our average retail investor. But foreign investors own 70% of TSMC's stock. Can we control the whole TSMC? The answer is probably still no. It's because these foreign investors are very diversified. Including European and American countries. Especially American investment institutions. Mutual Funds and Individuals. But in fact, it also includes Hong Kong. Singapore Government, Norwegian Central Bank. And even organizations around the world such as the Fallen Zone. And then there's the sum of individual investor holdings. These 70% of the shares are distributed globally. More than 7,000 foreign corporations and funds. Behind each of these organizations,. There are many foreign investors. And so. Even if foreign ownership is high. However, they are not uniformly concentrated in a single stock. or in the hands of a few. After all, who can do anything? What about the idea of unifying such a large group of people? Uh...no. I've forgotten about His Holiness. And the idea of unification doesn't matter. This man needs to be able to unite the ranks. Significant impact on TSMC with the implementation of the holding. Basically, it's still not going to happen. Same situation. In fact, Samsung in Korea. Or Alibaba before the foreign investors pulled out. It's a similar situation. Currently Samsung's largest shareholding is also in the US. But Samsung has not become American. Because the largest shareholding. Same as “Investment Institutions. And there are countless other clients behind it. High Foreign Ownership. Compare to the world's consensus. You're going to do well as a business. I'd like to invest in you. But back to the point. The sadder part is that. The money we make at TSMC. It's still a big part of it. Because of the dividend. It's just that the foreigners are making money out of it. But if you really want to be strict about the math.

Single Legal Entity. TSMC has the largest percentage of shareholding. In addition to TSMC itself. I think it's still our Executive Yuan. The National Development Fund Management Board. Is now at 6.38%.

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DgKowmSA17Q

今天的故事

就是要來介紹台積電

是怎麼從一個

默默無聞的小企業

逐漸成為全球半導體的領先企業

進而有了「護國神山」的稱號

除了因為它有世界級的成就以外

也因為大家都認定

它對這個世界

是不可或缺的存在

有助於保護我們中華民國的現狀

所以今天我們就來聊聊

這座護國神山！

還有網路上傳了很久的

台積電就是美積電

但事實真的是這樣嗎？

#臺灣 #台灣 #中華民國 #中國 #歷史 #tsmc #台積電 #半導體 #張忠謀 #護國神山 #美積電 #美國 #奈米 #晶片 #埃 #三星 #工研院 #行政院 #理工男 #新竹科學園區 #竹科 #國產

😉特別感謝5月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
